content: I just finished reading 'Being and Time' by Heidegger. It's mind-bending stuff! I've been really into philosophy lately, trying to make sense of everything before the baby arrives.
Congratulations on the upcoming arrival! That's an ambitious read for someone expecting their first child. How are you balancing your reading with all the preparations?
Thanks! It's a bit of a juggling act, but I find philosophy helps me process everything. Between painting the nursery and attending birthing classes, I squeeze in reading whenever I can.
That's a great way to approach it. What other philosophical works have you been enjoying?

I recently read 'The Ethics of Ambiguity' by de Beauvoir. It really spoke to me as I navigate this new chapter in my life. Speaking of new chapters, I've also taken up woodworking to make some furniture for the baby's room.
How wonderful! There's something so special about handmade items for a new baby. What have you made so far?
I've built a small bookshelf and I'm working on a rocking chair. It's been challenging but rewarding. I've also been experimenting with home brewing - made a nice amber ale last month.
You're quite the Renaissance person! Brewing, woodworking, and philosophy - that's an impressive range of interests. How did you get into home brewing?
My dad got me into it years ago. We used to brew together when I was in college. It's a nice way to relax after a long day at the SaaS company where I work as a Marketing Manager.
That's a lovely tradition to carry on. Do you have any favorite brews you've created?
My IPA is pretty popular among my friends. I'm thinking of trying a mushroom-infused beer next - I've been getting into mushroom foraging lately. Found some beautiful morels last weekend!
Mushroom foraging sounds fascinating! And mushroom beer? That's certainly unique. Have you done much research on safe foraging practices?
Absolutely. Safety first! I've been going out with an experienced forager and carrying a good field guide. It's become a bit of an obsession, to be honest.
It's great that you're being so careful. Foraging can be incredibly rewarding when done safely. Do you incorporate your finds into your cooking as well?
Oh yes! I made a fantastic risotto with the morels I found. Cooking is another passion of mine - I love experimenting with new recipes. It's a nice balance to my day job in marketing.
It sounds like you have a wonderful balance of creative and intellectual pursuits. With the baby coming, do you think you'll have to scale back on any of your hobbies?
I'm sure things will change, but I'm hoping to involve the little one in some of my interests as they grow. Maybe start them early on woodworking or philosophy!
That sounds like a wonderful plan. I'm sure your child will grow up in a rich, stimulating environment. Is there anything else you're particularly excited about as you prepare for parenthood?

I'm really looking forward to sharing my love of books and learning. I've already started collecting some of my favorite children's books. Oh, and I can't wait to see what kind of sense of humor they inherit - I do a bit of stand-up comedy on the side!
